title: They loved Trajan so much they rewrote the afterlife for him - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer discusses how the Roman Emperor Trajan was so revered that later Christians reinterpreted classical afterlife concepts to accommodate his legacy. This is a historical and philosophical commentary on cultural memory and adaptation.
writeup: In this conversation, historian Ada Palmer examines the posthumous reputation of the Roman emperor Trajan, focusing on how later Roman culture revised mythological and afterlife narratives to accommodate his memory. The case illustrates how a ruler's reputation can become powerful enough to bend a culture's existing religious and cosmological stories. The central takeaway: Trajan was regarded as one of Rome's most successful emperors, and his veneration extended beyond conventional honors to a reworking of how the afterlife itself was imagined.
